framing: Operations manager at Maersk Dubai applying to DP World logistics technology role

jd.title: Manager — Digital Solutions & Trade Automation
jd.style_note: Enterprise SaaS: structured, specific platform knowledge, trade/logistics domain requirements

requirements.text: 7+ years of experience in logistics, supply chain management, or trade facilitation
Experience with TOS (Terminal Operating Systems): Navis N4, TBA OPUS, or Kalmar TLS
Knowledge of trade documentation systems (bill of lading, letters of credit, customs declarations)
Experience with container tracking platforms and EDI/API integrations (INTTRA, CargoSmart, or TradeLens successor platforms)
Familiarity with UAE customs systems (Dubai Trade Portal, Mirsal 2) and Free Zone regulations
Project management skills with PMP or PRINCE2 certification
Experience with blockchain-based trade finance or smart B/L platforms (e.g., CargoX, Bolero)

responsibilities: Lead implementation of DP World's digital trade platform across 5 Middle East terminals
Drive automation of customs clearance and trade documentation workflows
Manage integration between TOS, shipping line systems, and government customs platforms
Coordinate with Dubai Customs and Free Zone authorities on regulatory compliance
Develop business cases for digital transformation initiatives in terminal operations

expected_output.summary: Logistics operations professional with 8 years of experience at Maersk, most recently Operations Manager at Jebel Ali Container Terminal (JACT). Managed daily container throughput of 4,000+ TEUs. Deep expertise in terminal operations, vessel planning, and Navis N4 TOS. Experience with EDI integrations between shipping lines, terminals, and customs systems. Led process improvement projects that reduced truck turnaround time by 22%. Seeking digital transformation role combining logistics domain expertise with technology platform management.

experience.title: Operations Manager — Container Terminal
bullets: Managed daily operations for Jebel Ali container terminal processing 4,000+ TEUs/day across 8 berths
Oversaw Navis N4 TOS configuration for vessel planning, yard management, and gate operations
Led EDI integration project connecting terminal systems with 12 major shipping lines and Dubai Customs (Mirsal 2)
Reduced truck turnaround time from 45 to 35 minutes through gate automation and appointment system implementation
Managed team of 25 operations staff across 3 shifts including planners, controllers, and equipment operators

experience.title: Terminal Planner (promoted from Operations Coordinator, 2020)
bullets: Planned vessel stowage and yard allocation for weekly vessel calls from Asia-Europe and intra-Gulf services
Operated Navis N4 for container tracking, hold/release management, and customs status updates
Processed trade documentation (bills of lading, delivery orders, customs declarations) for import/export containers
Coordinated with Dubai Trade Portal for electronic customs clearance and Free Zone documentation

certifications: APICS CSCP — Certified Supply Chain Professional (2023)
education: BSc Supply Chain Management — University of Sharjah (2014-2018)

jd_requirements: 7+ years logistics supply chain trade facilitation
TOS Navis N4 TBA OPUS Kalmar TLS terminal operating systems
trade documentation bill of lading letters of credit customs declarations
container tracking INTTRA CargoSmart TradeLens EDI API
UAE customs Dubai Trade Portal Mirsal 2 Free Zone regulations
blockchain trade finance CargoX Bolero smart B/L
deliberate_flaws: No TBA OPUS or Kalmar TLS experience — only Navis N4
No letters of credit experience (trade documentation was B/L and customs, not trade finance)
No CargoSmart experience, only basic INTTRA
No blockchain or smart B/L platform experience (CargoX, Bolero)
No PMP or PRINCE2 certification (has CSCP which is supply chain, not PM)
No API integration experience — managed EDI projects as stakeholder, not technical implementer
No digital platform product management experience (operations manager, not product/tech)
No Arabic language skills (Emirati university but international background)
Operations background — transition to 'Digital Solutions' requires technology management skills
TradeLens shut down in 2022 — JD references 'successor platforms' which are evolving
